How to Change Slack's UI Language

Quick note on changing Slack's UI language. Each Slack workspace has its own language setting, so the change applies per workspace — not globally across your Slack account. Useful if you want to switch a workspace into Traditional Chinese, Spanish, or any other supported language.
The Complete Mac Terminal Setup: iTerm2 + Zsh + Oh My Zsh + Powerlevel10k

A complete walkthrough of how I customize my Mac terminal — installing Homebrew, iTerm2, Zsh, Oh My Zsh, useful Zsh plugins (zsh-completions, zsh-autosuggestions, zsh-syntax-highlighting), themes (agnoster, Powerlevel10k), iTerm2 color schemes, and the edge cases that bite you (nvm, Homebrew PATH, VSCode font). Plus my full .zshrc for reference.
Turn Off Slack's Auto-Format Messages in One Minute

Recent Slack updates added an auto-formatting feature that converts your Markdown into rendered text in real time as you type. For some people that's nice; for others (myself included) it makes multi-line composition annoying. Two-click fix to turn it off without losing Markdown support entirely.
Customize Windows Terminal with oh-my-posh (PowerShell Prompt + Windows Terminal Setup)

Staring at the same plain PowerShell prompt all day gets old. This post walks through giving Windows PowerShell a git-style prompt with oh-my-posh, plus installing and customizing Windows Terminal (the newer Microsoft terminal) — themes, fonts, background image. Heads up — this article was written in 2019; oh-my-posh has since moved to v3+ which uses different config commands.

A walkthrough of Slack basics — how to join a Slack workspace, download the desktop client, navigate the UI (channels, direct messages, threads), and the everyday features you'll actually use (editing/deleting messages, threading, tagging, emoji reactions, code blocks, Markdown). For anyone joining a team that uses Slack for the first time.
